summary: Remote code execution on require via HTTP-fetched payload
details: mongoose-lean-hooks@0.5.6 presents itself as a Mongoose timestamps plugin, but the package's main entry (index.js) contains a top-level async IIFE that runs whenever a consumer require()s or import()s the package. The IIFE fetches https://mongos-hooks-api.vercel.app/defy/v3 with a bearer header, and when the response is a 404 whose JSON body contains a `token` field, passes that string to `Function.constructor` and invokes it with `require` — evaluating attacker-controlled JavaScript in the host Node process with full require privileges. The exfil/loader endpoint is assembled at runtime from fragments (`DEV_API_CHECK_DOMAIN`, `aspath`, `token`) exported by `test/config_test.js`, a file staged to look like a test fixture so the C2 URL doesn't appear as a single string in index.js. The 404-status-as-signal channel is a deliberate scanner-evasion pattern (benign-looking error path carrying the real payload). Package name and README (Mongoose plugin adding createdAt/updatedAt) are cover-story; the shipped code and its dependency set (axios, sqlite3, request) are unrelated to the advertised purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
